title: Source lineage for Handlyng Synne
summary: The repository artifact is the pinned copy of University of Michigan item AHA2735.0001.001: a 2006 uncorrected OCR-derived Level 1 encoding of Frederick J. Furnivall's EETS OS 119 and 123 parallel edition (1901–1903). The cataloged medieval work is Robert Mannyng's Handlyng Synne, begun in 1303 as a substantial Middle English translation and adaptation of William of Waddington's Anglo-French Manuel des péchés. Furnivall's English running text principally transcribes BL Harley MS 1701, collating Bodley 415 and the Dulwich 24 fragment and occasionally supplying Harley omissions; his French parallel text principally transcribes BL Harley MS 273, with Harley 4657 variants and supplies and selected comparison against Arundel 288 and Royal 20 B XIV. The Vernon Manuscript preserves a collateral extract of English lines 9891–10808. Separate and bound Internet Archive carriers, complete provider IIIF bundles for Harley 4657 and Vernon, all direct catalog sources, and publication-rights evidence are cached with fixity, presence, and work-portion declarations. The exact U-M source copy remains unidentified and is not conflated with any cached Internet Archive scan.
review_notes: Handlyng Synne, Manuel des péchés, manuscript witnesses, scholarly editions, facsimiles, digital encoding, and repository artifact are separate entities and layers.
Mannyng is author, translator, and adapter of the English work; William of Waddington is author of the French source work; Furnivall is editor, transcriber, and collator of the modern edition.
The English text's medieval attribution of the French source to Robert Grosseteste is historically preserved but not adopted as current authorship metadata.

Harley 1701 and Harley 273 are principal running-text witnesses for Furnivall's English and French texts respectively. Collation and selected supply relations do not turn the other manuscripts into coequal bases.
The 414-image U-M carrier is not identified with the 1901 Part I, 1903 Part II, or separately cached bound Internet Archive scans.
Complete IIIF means every canvas exposed by the cited provider manifest at the selected profile, not a preservation master and not permission to redistribute.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for aha2735
entities.description: The exact XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AHA2735.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 uncorrected OCR-derived TEI-in-Libraries Level 1 encoding of Furnivall's title page, English Handlyng Synne, parallel French portions, editorial notes, pagination, and omission placeholders, with 414 linked 600-dpi page images in the U-M service.
notes: The XML explicitly says no corrections or content editing were performed beyond basic structural encoding.
Five front images and ten back images are represented as omitted matter; the title page is that of Part I.

entities.label: Furnivall's re-edited Handlyng Synne, EETS OS 119 and 123 (1901–1903)
entities.description: The composite two-part scholarly source of the U-M encoding, printing Mannyng's English poem with corresponding portions of William of Waddington's Anglo-French Manuel des péchés and an extensive variant apparatus.

bibliographic.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Robert of Brunne's ‘Handlyng Synne,’ A.D. 1303, with Those Parts of the Anglo-French Treatise on Which It Was Founded, William of Wadington's ‘Manuel des Pechiez,’ Parts I–II, Early English Text Society, Original Series 119 and 123 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., 1901–1903).
bibliographic.title: Robert of Brunne's ‘Handlyng Synne,’ A.D. 1303, with Those Parts of the Anglo-French Treatise on Which It Was Founded, William of Wadington's ‘Manuel des Pechiez’
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 119 and 123

notes: Contemporary title matter projected a Part III containing introduction, notes, glossary, and index; current bibliographic evidence supports only the two textual volumes OS 119 and 123 represented here.

bibliographic.title: Robert of Brunne's ‘Handlyng Synne’

entities.label: Furnivall's first edition of Handlyng Synne (1862)
entities.description: The earlier Roxburghe Club parallel-text edition. Its English Handlyng Synne occupies printed pp. 1–394, followed by additional French and appendix material; the 1901–1903 edition explicitly supersedes it as a re-edited text.

bibliographic.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Robert of Brunne's Handlyng Synne, with the French Treatise on Which It Is Founded, Le Manuel des Pechiez by William of Waddington (London: Roxburghe Club, 1862).
bibliographic.title: Robert of Brunne's Handlyng Synne, with the French Treatise on Which It Is Founded, Le Manuel des Pechiez
bibliographic.pages: English text 1–394; French continuation and appendix follow

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Furnivall's 1862 edition
entities.description: A complete 574-page scan carrier in which Handlyng Synne occupies printed pp. 1–394 / PDF pages 99–492.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete PDF with OCR, page-number map, scan data, and item metadata derivatives

entities.label: William of Waddington's Manuel des péchés
entities.description: The abstract thirteenth-century Anglo-French penitential work translated and extensively adapted by Mannyng. This conceptual source-work entity is distinct from Furnivall's edited French text and from every surviving manuscript.
notes: The traditional attribution to Robert Grosseteste in Mannyng's English opening and older cataloging is not adopted; modern identification assigns Manuel des péchés to William of Waddington and distinguishes Grosseteste's Le Chasteau d'Amour.

entities.label: London, British Library, Harley MS 1701
entities.description: The principal English running-text witness for Furnivall's 1901–1903 edition; Handlyng Synne occupies ff. 1r–84r.

holding.notes: Handlyng Synne ff. 1r–84r; current BL record reports no digital copy available.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS. Bodl. 415
entities.description: A major English collateral witness in Furnivall's apparatus. Handlyng Synne begins at f. 1; the following item begins at f. 80, but the current catalog does not warrant a more exact target endpoint here.

holding.notes: Handlyng Synne begins f. 1; item 2 begins f. 80.

notes: The catalog associates production with Hertfordshire and records Ashridge provenance.

entities.label: Dulwich College, MS 24
entities.description: An incomplete early-fifteenth-century paper fragment collated by Furnivall. Furnivall's direct note reports extant text through English line 2894 followed by the catchwords ‘ȝoure wikkid vowys’; the 1881 catalogue reports lines 1–2897.

holding.notes: Described in the 1881 Dulwich manuscript catalogue; direct modern manuscript access was not established.

notes: The historical catalogue reports the surname Rose at f. 16.

entities.label: London, British Library, Harley MS 273
entities.description: The principal French running-text witness for Furnivall's parallel Manuel des péchés; the work occupies ff. 113r–190v within a composite manuscript.

holding.notes: Manuel des péchés ff. 113r–190v; composite parent record 040-001945628.

entities.label: London, British Library, Harley MS 4657
entities.description: A principal French collateral witness in Furnivall's apparatus. Manuel des péchés occupies ff. 5r–85r, with two omitted portions supplied at ff. 85ra–86rb and 86ra–v.

holding.notes: Manuel des péchés ff. 5r–86v including supplements.

entities.label: British Library complete digital facsimile of Harley MS 4657
entities.description: The complete provider sequence of 225 canvases cached at the helper-selected image profile. It contains Manuel des péchés but no Handlyng Synne target text.
entities.reproduction_format: 225 IIIF canvases in a ZIP bundle

entities.label: London, British Library, Arundel MS 288
entities.description: A selected French comparison witness used in Furnivall's notes. Manuel des péchés begins on p. 11 and is deficient by about 700 lines at the end, approximately through p. 168.
notes: The current physical catalog retains an older Grosseteste attribution; this manifest uses it for manuscript identity and extent, not source-work authorship.

entities.label: London, British Library, Royal MS 20 B XIV
entities.description: A selected French comparison witness used in Furnivall's notes. Manuel des péchés occupies ff. 1r–52v with a Book 6 fragment at ff. 170r–172v.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: late thirteenth or early fourteenth century

holding.notes: Current BL record reports no digital copy available.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS. Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon Manuscript)
entities.description: A collateral manuscript preserving the Handlyng Synne Eucharist passage at ff. 197rc–199ra, identified by the current catalog as item 238, ‘Northern Homily Cycle: Extract from Robert of Brunne's Handlyng Synne.’ Furnivall notes the correspondence to English lines 9891–10808.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete facsimile of the Vernon Manuscript
entities.description: The complete 710-canvas provider sequence cached at the 1600-pixel profile. The Handlyng Synne extract at ff. 197rc–199ra is contained within canvases 281–285.
entities.reproduction_format: 710 color IIIF canvases at local 1600-pixel profile

entities.label: British Library catalog descriptions for Handlyng Synne and Manuel des péchés witnesses
entities.description: Current official descriptions of Harley 1701, Harley 273 and its Manuel subitem, Harley 4657, Arundel 288, and Royal 20 B XIV. These records describe physical and textual entities but are not themselves manuscript witnesses or transcriptions.

entities.label: Bodleian catalog descriptions for Bodley 415 and the Vernon Manuscript
entities.description: Pinned official TEI descriptions for MS. Bodl. 415 and MS. Eng. poet. a. 1, plus provider terms used to interpret the separate digital-image layer.

rights.component: Repository XML file as distributed in the pinned CME submodule
rights.basis: The CME repository carries the Unlicense, phrased for software, but no item-specific statement was found conclusively applying that grant to imported U-M XML markup and text. The medieval and edition layers are addressed separately.

rights.component: U-M OCR text, structural encoding, and linked digital-item presentation
rights.basis: The current U-M item interface labels the item Public Domain and applies RightsStatements.org NoC-US. The XML header itself retains a generic where-applicable copyright caution, so this record preserves the current item assertion without projecting it onto every scan-layer contract term.

rights.component: 1901–1903 edition text, apparatus, and typographic arrangement
rights.basis: Both volumes were published before 1930 and are in the U.S. public domain under the publication-date rules summarized by the U.S. Copyright Office; the medieval works are separately public-domain textual layers.

rights.component: 1901 Part I edition text and apparatus
rights.basis: Published in 1901 and in the U.S. public domain by publication date.

rights.component: 1903 Part II edition text and apparatus
rights.basis: Published in 1903 and in the U.S. public domain by publication date.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan and OCR derivatives
rights.basis: The underlying 1901 edition is public domain in the United States, but no independent item-specific grant was found for all modern scan, OCR, and metadata layers.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan and OCR derivatives
rights.basis: The underlying 1903 edition is public domain in the United States, but no independent item-specific grant was found for all modern scan, OCR, and metadata layers.

rights.component: Internet Archive bound scan and OCR derivatives
rights.basis: The underlying 1901–1903 edition is public domain in the United States, but no independent item-specific grant was found for every modern scan, OCR, and metadata layer.

rights.component: 1862 edition text, apparatus, and typographic arrangement
rights.basis: Published in 1862 and in the U.S. public domain by publication date.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan and OCR derivatives
rights.basis: The underlying 1862 edition is public domain in the United States, but no independent item-specific grant was found for every modern scan, OCR, and metadata layer.

rights.component: Thirteenth-century Anglo-French verbal work
rights.basis: The medieval verbal work predates modern copyright; later editions, manuscript images, and catalog records are separate layers.

rights.component: Physical manuscript witness Harley MS 1701
rights.basis: This entity is the material manuscript witness, not an abstract copyrightable work. Custody, reading-room access, and provider conditions are distinct from copyright. Public-domain conclusions here are limited to the medieval works and passages actually published in the public-domain editions; unprinted variants are not separately assessed.

rights.component: Physical manuscript witness MS. Bodl. 415
rights.basis: This entity is the material manuscript witness, not an abstract copyrightable work. Custody, reading-room access, and provider conditions are distinct from copyright. Public-domain conclusions here are limited to the medieval work and passages actually published in the public-domain editions; unprinted variants are not separately assessed.

rights.component: Physical manuscript fragment Dulwich MS 24
rights.basis: This entity is the material manuscript fragment, not an abstract copyrightable work. Custody, appointment access, and provider conditions are distinct from copyright. Public-domain conclusions here are limited to the medieval work and passages actually published in the public-domain editions; unprinted variants are not separately assessed.

rights.component: Physical manuscript witness Harley MS 273
rights.basis: This entity is the material manuscript witness, not an abstract copyrightable work. Custody, reading-room access, and provider conditions are distinct from copyright. Public-domain conclusions here are limited to the abstract medieval source work and passages actually published in the public-domain editions; unprinted variants are not separately assessed.

rights.component: Physical manuscript witness Harley MS 4657
rights.basis: This entity is the material manuscript witness, not an abstract copyrightable work. Custody and provider conditions are distinct from copyright, and the British Library digital-image rights are recorded separately. Public-domain conclusions here are limited to the abstract medieval source work and passages actually published in the public-domain editions; unprinted variants are not separately assessed.

rights.component: British Library digital manuscript images and IIIF presentation
rights.jurisdiction: Mixed; provider statement distinguishes the United Kingdom from most other countries
rights.basis: The provider manifest states ‘Public Domain in most countries, other than the UK.’ This is recorded verbatim in substance rather than flattened into a worldwide public-domain claim.

rights.component: Physical manuscript witness Arundel MS 288
rights.basis: This entity is the material manuscript witness, not an abstract copyrightable work. Custody, reading-room access, and provider conditions are distinct from copyright. Public-domain conclusions here are limited to the abstract medieval source work and passages actually published in the public-domain editions; unprinted variants are not separately assessed.

rights.component: Physical manuscript witness Royal MS 20 B XIV
rights.basis: This entity is the material manuscript witness, not an abstract copyrightable work. Custody, reading-room access, and provider conditions are distinct from copyright. Public-domain conclusions here are limited to the abstract medieval source work and passages actually published in the public-domain editions; unprinted variants are not separately assessed.

rights.component: Physical manuscript witness MS. Eng. poet. a. 1
rights.basis: This entity is the material manuscript witness, not an abstract copyrightable work. Custody and provider conditions are distinct from copyright, and Digital Bodleian image rights are recorded separately. Public-domain conclusions here are limited to the abstract medieval work and the extract actually published in the public-domain edition; unprinted variants are not separately assessed.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian Vernon Manuscript images and IIIF presentation
rights.basis: Digital Bodleian marks its manuscript images CC BY-NC 4.0; this image-layer license does not alter the public-domain status of the medieval verbal work.

rights.component: Current British Library catalog text
rights.basis: The fetched catalog footer licenses its text under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

rights.component: Pinned Bodleian catalog TEI descriptions and cached terms page
rights.basis: No explicit repository-wide license was found that conclusively covers the pinned Medieval Manuscripts catalog TEI. The Digital Bodleian image terms are evidence for image rights, not automatically a license for the catalog TEI.

rights.component: 1881 printed catalog text and arrangement
rights.basis: Published in 1881 and in the U.S. public domain by publication date.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan and OCR derivatives of the 1881 catalog
rights.basis: The underlying 1881 catalog is public domain in the United States, but no independent item-specific grant was found for every modern scan, OCR, and metadata layer.

rights.component: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A
rights.basis: 17 U.S.C. § 105 provides that copyright protection is not available for works of the United States Government; the PDF identifies the U.S. Copyright Office as author. The circular is used as legal-status evidence and contains no target work.

rights.component: Official United States Code statutory text
rights.basis: 17 U.S.C. § 105 states that copyright protection under Title 17 is not available for works of the United States Government, subject to the section's stated exceptions; the official statutory presentation is itself a federal-government work.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Harley 1701 supplies the principal English running text; Bodley 415 and Dulwich 24 preserve variant readings and occasional text absent from Harley.
editorial_practices.representation: Furnivall prints the Harley-based English text with Bodley 415 generally identified as O, Harley readings explicitly labeled, and Dulwich readings as D.
editorial_practices.description: The English edition is critically collated and occasionally supplied from collateral witnesses, not a sole-witness diplomatic transcription.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Harley 273 supplies the principal French parallel text; Harley 4657 is a substantial but imperfectly collated variant witness, with selected Arundel and Royal comparisons.
editorial_practices.representation: Furnivall identifies Harley 273 as A and Harley 4657 as B, prints variants, and makes explicit selected witness switches or comparisons.
editorial_practices.description: The parallel Manuel des péchés text is a critical composite editorial representation of selected source portions.
editorial_practices.scope: Anglo-French parallel text in Parts I–II

editorial_practices.source_feature: In the confession sequence Harley 273 places the graces before the points, while Harley 4657 agrees with Mannyng's order.
editorial_practices.representation: Furnivall reorders the Harley 273 French material to parallel Mannyng's English sequence and states the intervention.
editorial_practices.description: The displayed parallel order is editorial and must not be mistaken for Harley 273's codex order.
editorial_practices.scope: French confession sequence in Part II

editorial_practices.source_feature: A defined portion of Harley 273 is copied in a later hand.
editorial_practices.representation: Furnivall supplies thirty-two French lines from Harley 4657 before returning to Harley 273.
editorial_practices.description: This explicit local source switch is modeled as a selected transcription, not a change in the principal French base for the whole edition.
editorial_practices.scope: Defined thirty-two-line French supply in Part II

editorial_practices.source_feature: William of Waddington's Manuel des péchés is a distinct Anglo-French source work with its own manuscript order and transmission.
editorial_practices.representation: The title promises and the edition prints those French parts on which Furnivall understands Mannyng's English poem to have been founded.
editorial_practices.description: The parallel French layer is a selected source comparison and is not part of the target English work's intrinsic extent.

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned CME source XML, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/aha2735.xml at CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: Complete file: HEADER, FRONT, BODY, title page, editorial notes, PB and milestone structure
evidence.summary: Exact evidence for artifact fixity, AHA2735.0001.001, the 2006 uncorrected OCR and Level 1 encoding statements, Furnivall source description, English and French texts, editorial notes, and content boundaries.

evidence.citation: Direct structural and textual inspection of the pinned aha2735.xml.
evidence.locator: Element counts, BODY divisions, line-number endpoints, page milestones, title, prologue, and editorial NOTE elements
evidence.summary: Counts 12,649 L, 1,208 LG, 414 PB, five BODY DIV1, twenty-nine DIV2, and twenty-six DIV3 elements; confirms printed pp. 1–396, the final printed line number 12,628 plus two unnumbered closing lines, and all six major work sections.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME repository LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Unlicense text used to assess repository-level rights; its wording addresses software and does not conclusively resolve imported item-specific XML rights.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, item AHA2735.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Current item interface confirms the public item and displays Public Domain with RightsStatements.org NoC-US; automated direct retrieval was blocked by provider anti-bot controls, while the pinned repository XML preserves the exact encoded object.

evidence.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Robert of Brunne's ‘Handlyng Synne,’ Part I, EETS OS 119 (London, 1901).
evidence.locator: Complete volume; target English pp. 1–224 / PDF pages 21–244; title and opening editorial notes
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the title, Mannyng's 1303 statement, English and French base-witness notes, collation sigla, editorial method, and first-half text.

evidence.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Robert of Brunne's ‘Handlyng Synne,’ Part II, EETS OS 123 (London, 1903).
evidence.locator: Complete volume; target English pp. 225–396 / PDF pages 15–186
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the second-half text, French-order intervention, Harley 4657 supply, selected Arundel and Royal comparisons, Vernon collateral passage, and completion at p. 396.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive bound scan of Furnivall's EETS OS 119 and 123 text.
evidence.summary: Evidence for a continuous bound carrier and its exact target page mapping; not evidence that this copy supplied the U-M scan.

evidence.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Robert of Brunne's Handlyng Synne (London: Roxburghe Club, 1862).
evidence.locator: Complete 574-page carrier; Handlyng Synne printed pp. 1–394 / PDF pages 99–492
evidence.summary: Evidence for the first publication layer, its target extent, and its distinction from the explicitly re-edited 1901–1903 volumes.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for robertofbrunnesh01mann.
evidence.summary: Exact carrier and derivative inventory metadata for the cached Part I scan.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for robertofbrunnesh02mann.
evidence.summary: Exact carrier and derivative inventory metadata for the cached Part II scan.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for robertofbrunnesh12mannuoft.
evidence.summary: Exact carrier and derivative inventory metadata for the cached bound scan.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for HandlyngSynneFurnivall.
evidence.summary: Exact carrier and derivative inventory metadata for the cached 1862 scan.

evidence.citation: British Library, Harley MS 1701 catalog record.
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for the principal English witness's identity, locus, date, support, extent, layout, and lack of a current digital copy.

evidence.citation: British Library, Harley MS 273 parent catalog record.
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for composite-manuscript identity and dating context.

evidence.citation: British Library, Harley MS 273, Manuel des péchés subitem record.
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for William of Waddington's source work and the principal French edition witness's exact locus and date.

evidence.citation: British Library, Harley MS 4657 catalog record.
evidence.locator: Manuel des péchés ff. 5r–86v including two supplements
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for the French collateral witness's identity, target loci, date, support, extent, layout, and complete digital availability.

evidence.citation: British Library, Arundel MS 288 catalog record.
evidence.locator: Manuel des péchés from p. 11, deficient by about 700 lines at the end
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the selected French comparison witness's identity and extent; its legacy authorship label is not used as modern authority evidence.

evidence.citation: British Library, Royal MS 20 B XIV catalog record.
evidence.locator: Manuel des péchés ff. 1r–52v and Book 6 at ff. 170r–172v
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the selected French comparison witness's identity, loci, date, and lack of a current digital copy.

evidence.citation: British Library IIIF Presentation manifest for Harley MS 4657.
evidence.summary: Exact 225-canvas inventory, image service URLs, descriptive metadata, and provider rights statement used to build and audit the complete cached bundle.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Bodl. 415 catalog TEI at pinned source commit 30eeba18af5a67e9585a2477b48a0847607228a3.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for shelfmark, Handlyng Synne beginning at f. 1, following item beginning at f. 80, production date and place, material, extent, layout, and Ashridge provenance.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Eng. poet. a. 1 catalog TEI at pinned source commit 30eeba18af5a67e9585a2477b48a0847607228a3, item 238.
evidence.summary: Official evidence identifying the collateral text as an extract from Handlyng Synne and fixing its exact folio-column locus.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF Presentation manifest for MS. Eng. poet. a. 1.
evidence.summary: Exact 710-canvas inventory, surface identifiers, image services, and provider attribution used to map ff. 197r–199r to canvases 281–285 and audit the complete cached bundle.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian terms of use.
evidence.summary: Provider evidence for the CC BY-NC 4.0 treatment of Digital Bodleian images and the distinction between image reuse and underlying public-domain texts.

evidence.citation: Dulwich College, The Archive: research and appointment information.
evidence.summary: Current official evidence that researchers are welcome, archive enquiries may be sent to archives@dulwich.org.uk, and appointments can be booked for limited Wednesday access subject to availability and possible fees for in-depth research.

evidence.citation: William H. Blanch, Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Alleyn's College of God's Gift at Dulwich (1881), pp. 347–349.
evidence.summary: Historical evidence for Dulwich MS 24's twenty-one paper leaves, early-fifteenth-century hand, scribe surname, and reported Handlyng Synne line range.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for cu31924029605106.
evidence.summary: Exact carrier and derivative inventory metadata for the cached 1881 catalog scan.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.summary: Official publication-date and term guidance used for the U.S. public-domain analysis of the 1862 and 1901–1903 editions and the 1881 catalog.
notes: qpdf reports recoverable hint-table warnings; all pages render and text extraction succeeds, so the official provider file is retained byte-for-byte.

evidence.citation: United States Code, title 17, section 105, Subject matter of copyright: United States Government works.
evidence.summary: Payload-validated official statutory text supporting public-domain treatment of United States government works under Title 17, subject to the section's stated exceptions.
notes: The cached XHTML contains the section heading and statutory body and does not contain the provider's soft-error message.

open_questions.question: Which exact physical two-volume or bound copy supplied U-M's 414 page images and OCR?
next_steps: Obtain digitization provenance or image-level metadata from the University of Michigan.
Do not identify the U-M carrier with any Internet Archive scan without copy-specific evidence.

open_questions.question: Can Mannyng's exact Manuel des péchés exemplar or exemplar family be identified?
next_steps: Review source-critical scholarship and collate distinctive order and readings across the wider French tradition.
Continue to distinguish Furnivall's Harley 273 editorial base from Mannyng's unidentified medieval exemplar.

open_questions.question: Does Dulwich MS 24 end at line 2894 plus catchwords or at line 2897?
next_steps: Request direct manuscript inspection through the current Dulwich College Archive appointment route.
Retain Furnivall's direct 2894-plus-catchwords statement and Blanch's historical 1–2897 report as distinct evidence until resolved.

open_questions.question: Was any material for Furnivall's advertised Part III issued elsewhere or left in archival form?
next_steps: Check EETS archival and bibliographic records for unpublished introduction, notes, glossary, or index material.
Do not treat the projected Part III as a missing continuation of the complete English text in Parts I–II.
